t_en: Let me think... Oh, that's right, there's a child that keeps bugging me to teach it bartending. I told it to wait till it's a little older, but it's probably secretly practicing by the <color=#dbc291ff>bar counter</color>, tweet!

t_en: Let me think... Oh, that's right, the <color=#dbc291ff>guest area's sofa</color> is soft and warm. We sometimes take a break there, but there's always a child that simply doesn't want to leave, tweet!

t_en: Let me think... Oh, that's right, I heard from a bird that there's a naughty child that <color=#dbc291ff>has recently been scaring people with a dinosaur. Even Brother Hanu has grumbled about it</color>, tweet!

t_ch: 让我想想…对了，有个孩子是钟表小子的粉丝，只要看到钟表就往人家怀里钻，<color=#dbc291ff>路上的时钟先生们</color>都很为难啾～
t_en: Let me think... Oh, that's right, there's a child that's a fan of Clockie. It just runs into their arms every time it sees a clock. <color=#dbc291ff>The clocks along the road</color> must be at a loss, tweet!

t_ch: 让我想想…对了，<color=#dbc291ff>有个全是小方块的房间，镜子里还有可怕的人影</color>，如果有孩子跑到那里去，可就糟糕了啾～
t_en: Let me think... Oh, that's right, <color=#dbc291ff>in a room filled with small squares, there are frightening figures that appear in the mirror</color>. It would be awful if a child got in there, tweet!

t_ch: 让我想想…对了，<color=#dbc291ff>那个方方正正的房间，里面的石头都又大又整齐</color>，有个热爱建筑的孩子，可能还在那里学习啾～
t_en: Let me think... Oh, that's right, <color=#dbc291ff>in that square room with its big and immaculate stones</color>, there's a child who's passionate about architecture, likely still trying to learn something there, tweet!

t_ch: 让我想想…对了，<color=#dbc291ff>有个会自动变形的房间</color>，没有人家这样成熟稳重的小鸟指引，孩子们一进去就很容易迷路啾～
t_en: Let me think... Oh, that's right, <color=#dbc291ff>there's a room that structurally shifts on its own</color>. Without someone mature and poised like me, children are bound to get lost if they enter, tweet!

t_ch: 让我想想…对了，<color=#dbc291ff>有个堆满了镜子的房间，里面不时会传出稚嫩的琴声</color>，想必是那个好学的孩子弄的动静啾～
t_en: Let me think... Oh, that's right, <color=#dbc291ff>there's a room full of mirrors, and sometimes dissonant piano tunes can be heard from within</color>. I bet it's that studious child that's causing the noise, tweet!

t_ch: 让我想想…对了，<color=#dbc291ff>听说镜子房间里有面特殊的镜子</color>，有个爱美的孩子，想必它肯定不会错过这面镜子啾～
t_en: Let me think... Oh, that's right, <color=#dbc291ff>I hear the mirror room has a special mirror</color>. We've got a vain child that definitely won't want to miss out on this mirror, tweet!

t_ch: 让我想想…对了，有好几个孩子都跑到<color=#dbc291ff>镜子房间</color>去了，有个活泼的孩子自告奋勇去接它们，它应该就在<color=#dbc291ff>入口</color>等着啾～
t_en: Let me think... Oh, that's right, quite a few children went into the <color=#dbc291ff>mirror room</color>. A lively child has volunteered to fetch them — They're probably waiting at the <color=#dbc291ff>entrance</color>, tweet!

t_ch: 还是算了吧。

t_ch: 小鸟们都很调皮，稍微不注意就会跑去其他地方…还是麻烦客官先把那孩子带回来吧！啾啾！

t_ch: 啾！客官要是想聊聊天，随时都可以来找人家啾。
t_en: Tweet! Feel free to come find me anytime if you want to chat, tweet.

t_ch: 啾！多亏了客官，才没让这几个调皮的孩子打扰到其他客人啾。

t_ch: 这是人家一点小小的心意，客官可千万不要嫌弃啾。
t_en: This is a token of my appreciation, I hope you won't scoff, tweet.

t_ch: 啾！多亏有客官您在，总算把这些小捣蛋鬼给找回来了啾。

t_ch: 啾！多亏有客官您在，总算把这些小捣蛋鬼给找回来了啾。
t_en: Tweet! It's all thanks to you that these mischievous little troublemakers were finally found, tweet.

t_ch: 来，这是人家特地为您准备的礼物，请务必收下，以后也多多关照啾。
t_en: Here, this is a present I've specially prepared for you, you must take it. I look forward to our future encounters, tweet.

t_ch: 啾！客官想聊聊天吗？人家一直都在啾。
t_en: Tweet! You wish to chat? I've been waiting here always, tweet.

t_ch: 我看见凝固的时光流淌，满城灯火辉映举目蛮荒\n群星浮游于梦与梦之间，在神秘的忆海漾开微浪
t_en: I see the flow of time frozen, a city full of lights shining on the gaze of the savage.\nStars float between dreams, sending ripples across the mysterious sea of memories.

t_ch: 我看见行经至此的旅者，与我同将壮丽绝景仰望\n原初之梦如此令人神往，你可情愿在此驻足徜徉……
t_en: I see travelers who come here, admiring the magnificent scenery with me.\nThe primitive dream is so truly mesmerizing, would you rest your journey and linger here...

t_ch: 你是诗人吗？
t_en: Are you a poet?

t_ch: 感谢你的赞美，我只是个偶尔喜欢卖弄文墨的诗歌爱好者，面对如此瑰丽奇景，难免有些心绪澎湃……
t_en: Thank you for your praise, but I'm just a dabbler of poetry who occasionally likes to show off. It's natural that one's emotions would be stirred when overwhelmed by such a resplendent view...

t_ch: 你讲话挺怪的…
t_en: You talk weird...

t_ch: 哈哈，见笑了。不过面对如此瑰丽奇景，我难免有些心绪澎湃……
t_en: Haha, I must sound ridiculous. It's only natural that one's emotions would be stirred when overwhelmed by such a resplendent view...

t_ch: 说人话。
t_en: Speak human please.

t_ch: 呃…抱歉，我只是一时有感而发……
t_en: Err... Apologies, I was just waxing poetic from the heart...

t_ch: 我是说…这座流梦城寨当真是个美妙又奇妙的地方。
t_en: I mean to say... This Dreamflux Fortress is truly a beautiful and wondrous place.

t_ch: 瞧，那忆质流淌凝聚的漩涡，那迷人的曲线、圆滑的折角…如此相似的两个{RUBY_B#梦境}世界{RUBY_E#}互为倒影，星光浮游其间、辉映鎏金灯火。
t_en: Look, that vortex of coalesced memoria flow, that mesmerizing contour, the smoothly rounded corners... These two similar {RUBY_B#Dreamscape}worlds{RUBY_E#} cast reflections on each other, floating starlight shining through like gilded radiance.

t_ch: 原初的梦境充满了迷人的矛盾——岌岌可危中的安稳、荒芜破败下的生机、困窘狭缝里的自由……
t_en: The Primitive Dreamscape was filled with enchanting contradictions — peace amidst precarious predicaments, life beneath desolate destruction, freedom between the crevices of adversity...

t_ch: 这里就像一块未经打磨的原石，而我已按捺不住自己的想象——想象着如何将其雕琢成美玉，就如将质朴语词勾连为精巧的诗……
t_en: This place is like a rough stone, and I can't suppress my own imagination — about how to sculpt it into a fine jade, to connect plain words into an exquisite poem...

t_ch: 可以聊聊吗？
t_en: Are you up for a chat?

t_ch: 当然，如果你有任何疑问，我将很乐意解答。
t_en: Of course, if you have any questions, I'd be more than happy to answer them.

t_ch: 你是做什么的？
t_en: What do you do?

t_ch: 我是受雇于隐夜鸫家系的筑梦师…至少明面上是。
t_en: I am the Dreamweaver hired by the Nightingale Family... or at least I am in name.

t_ch: 我曾是个漫游星间的雕塑家，帮助家族完成了一位「调弦师」的塑像，继而忝列贵客之流，受到了家族的邀约。
t_en: I was once a sculptor roaming the stars, and helped The Family complete a Chordmaster statue, subsequently becoming their honored guest and invited to The Family as a result.

t_ch: 接到隐夜鸫的盛情邀请后，我与好友一同来到匹诺康尼，投身筑梦事业。
t_en: After receiving the Nightingales' cordial invitation, I came to Penacony with my friends and embarked on a Dreamweaving career.

t_ch: 只是于我而言，那盛大美梦始终…抱歉，是我多言了，你不必在意。
t_en: It's just that to me, that grand dream eventually... Apologies, I've spoken too much, please don't mind me.

t_ch: 你为什么留在流梦礁？
t_en: Why have you decided to stay in the Dreamflux Reef?

t_ch: 我因令人惊恐的意外到访此处，并将理想与梦寄放于此——我希望将这里建设成理想中的模样。
t_en: I came to this place because of a horrific accident, and pinned my ideals and dream on this place — I hope to build this place in the image of my ideals.

t_ch: 这里让我想起了故乡，那是一个蛮荒的星球，有着无尽的黄沙、丰沃的绿洲与不熄的火焰。
t_en: This place reminds me of my home. It is a barren planet with boundless golden sand, vibrant oases and flames that don't extinguish.

t_ch: 那是个落后、粗犷的星球，却蕴含无限生机，却能包容一切自由，人人心怀浪漫的渴望——就像流梦礁一样。
t_en: But on that primitive, savage planet, bountiful life prevails, and it is inclusive of all freedoms, where everyone's hearts brim with romantic desire— just like Dreamflux Reef.

t_ch: 在我的宏愿之中，梦境当如此处，应自由地生长，结出美好的果实，长成如诗的仙境，而我会为之倾尽心血。
t_en: In my grand ambition, a Dreamscape should be as such: Where life is free to blossom into beautiful fruit, into a sanctuary regaled in poems, and I will spare no effort in achieving this.

t_ch: 我深知此非坦途，前路必将艰辛漫长、无法仅凭理论与知识畅行其上，但我愿将余生投注于此，见证它生发成长……
t_en: I know it won't be easy, and the path ahead will be arduous and long, that it cannot be built with theories and knowledge alone, but I'm willing to spend my life to achieve this, to witness its growth...

t_ch: 先聊到这吧。
t_en: That's enough chit-chat for now.

t_ch: 好的，那么请自便吧。
t_en: Okay, you do you then.

t_ch: 我先走了，拜拜。
t_en: I've got to go. Bye.

t_ch: 再见，愿你也能在此处找到诗意与浪漫。
t_en: Goodbye, may you also find the poetry and romanticism like I have here.

t_ch: 又见面了…你也沉浸于这壮丽奇观之中了吗？
t_en: We meet again... Are you also immersed in the grandiosity of this sight?

t_ch: 我一直认为，所谓筑梦，筑的不止是梦，更是做梦之人的心灵。
t_en: I've always felt that dreamweaving isn't just about dreams, but also the soul of the one who creates them.

t_ch: 它们起初就像这梦境一样荒芜索瑟，但只要精心雕琢、悉心教养，终究能够变作流光溢彩的美梦。
t_en: They start out barren and empty like this Dreamscape, but with meticulous carving and careful nurturing, they will one day become a beautiful dream of kaleidoscopic lights.

t_ch: 我如此坚信着…却不知道自己选择的道路是否正确。但幸运的是，如今我获得了一个自证的契机——
t_en: My belief is resolute... but I don't know if my path is correct. Luckily though, I've been given a chance to prove myself...

t_ch: 在这里，蛮荒中暗含生机、无序中透出安宁…我可以将它变为理想中的乌托邦、变为货真价实的「美梦」。
t_en: Here, where life is concealed within wilderness, where slivers of peace emerge from disorder... I can turn them into an ideal utopia, into a true dream.

t_ch: 人当自由、物应平等，梦中的灵魂，也将获得真正的幸福与和平……
t_en: When freedom and the material are equal to people, the souls in dreams will also earn true happiness and peace...

t_ch: 我曾用心血浇筑家族的美梦，如今却发现，那不过是个金碧辉煌的谎言。
t_en: I once slogged for The Family's dream, but now I have discovered that it is but a gilded lie.

t_ch: 我从旧日的理想中惊醒，怅然若失，不知何去何从……
t_en: I was shocked awake from my old ideals, at a loss, aimless...

t_ch: 被埋藏的梦，诞生于命途交汇\n被遗忘的梦，匿迹于物欲横流
t_en: A buried dream, birthing an encounter with destiny.\nA forgotten dream, hidden in a stream of materialism.

t_ch: 不可见的宿星引我来此，褪去锁枷拘囚\n被唤醒的热忱疾声高呼，方知渴念已久
t_en: An unseen star guided me here, breaking the fetters that shackled me.\nA roused fervor cries out, a realization of a long-muted desire.

t_ch: 我曾于矫揉的幸福里寻觅幸福，在虚假的自由中探求自由\n却只见金纸压垮了黄金的脊梁，见声色泥泞了谐声的鸣奏
t_en: I once sought happiness in arranged happiness, freedom in false freedom.\nYet only saw gold spines crushed by gold paper, sounds that sullied the melodies of harmony.

t_ch: 今我离群独步、踽踽难断去留\n犹似行舟迷雾、前途未可筹谋
t_en: Now I walk alone, my steps not knowing to stay or go.\nHeld hostage by fogged hesitation, a future that cannot be fathomed.

t_ch: 焦渴中花苞希冀阵雨泽润\n烈阳下旅者惦念浓荫庇佑
t_en: Parched flower buds hope for a shower of rain.\nTravelers beneath the sweltering sun yearn for shrouded shade.

t_ch: 而我如此渴念，陈腐的终将陈腐于陈腐的棺柩\n而我如此渴念，鲜活的终将鲜活于鲜活的洪流
t_en: And I long, the dead to remain dead in a dead casket.\nAnd I long, the living to remain living in a stream of life.

t_ch: 那人人向往的美梦，不过是个畸形的造物，暗藏着多少凶险与阴霾……
t_en: The dream that people look forward to is nothing but a twisted creature, lurking with myriad dangers and mist...

t_ch: 未入梦时，曾憧憬美梦；方入梦时，美梦竟成空\n入梦愈久，愈心生惶恐；入梦愈深，愈惫怠难行
t_en: Before diving into a dream, once yearning for one. Once dived into, the dream be a void.\nThe longer in a dream, the more paranoid the heart. The deeper in a dream, the more draining the body.

t_ch: 我梦见奏乐者端坐空中楼阁，傲慢自得\n我梦见逐波者囚拘欲望枷锁，纵情声色
t_en: I dream of musicians seated atop floating pavilions, lofty and smug.\nI dream of seekers caught in shackles of desire, reveling in worldly wants.

t_ch: 我梦见呻吟掩埋于糖浆，泥泞污浊\n我梦见真心困死于形骸，可鄙可唾
t_en: I dream of moans buried in syrup, mired and foul.\nI dream of bared hearts dying in their mortal shells, despicable and contemptible.

t_ch: 我梦见繁华光景、灯火通明，疯狂与混沌滋生于阴影\n我梦见苦痛魂灵、憔悴多病，美梦的疗救痛苦且致命
t_en: I dream of prosperous flourish, lights radiating, insanity and chaos spawned in shadow.\nI dream of suffering souls, emaciated and sick, the dream's healing, pain and death.

t_ch: 于是我蓦然惊醒——
t_en: And so I wake up in a start...

t_ch: 恒久的幸福从未诞生于此，短见的欢愉总在人心痴缠\n闪耀的星辰难逃寂灭暗淡，神圣的谐乐早已荒腔走板
t_en: Eternal happiness was never borne of here, fleeting joy so alluring to the heart.\nScintillating stars struggling to flee solitary doom, the sacred harmony long lost in the cacophony.

t_ch: 在这美梦之中，如何继续酣眠\n星辉失却颜色，霓虹过于耀眼
t_en: In this dream, how does one sleep soundly.\nThe radiant stars faded, the rainbow saturated.

t_ch: 柔声蜜语，鸩毒比蜜甜\n宝器珠光，自缚于绸缎
t_en: Soft whispers and honeyed words, bitter poison sweeter than saccharine.\nTreasures and gems, wrapped in satin.

t_ch: 于是我蓦然惊醒——\n耽于美梦者醉死梦中，无缘黎明
t_en: And so I wake up in a start...\nThe dreamer drowning in his own dream, never seeing the light of daybreak.

t_ch: 这原初的梦境，蕴藏着无限可能性。在这里，错误的得以被纠正，缺失的能够被补充……
t_en: This Primitive Dreamscape contains infinite possibilities. Here, mistakes can be corrected, and inadequacies can be patched up...

t_ch: 我自午夜美梦中惊醒，坠入愈深沉眠\n鸢尾花香随灯火暗淡，唯余清苦回甘
t_en: I startle awake from my twilight dream, descending into a deeper slumber.\nThe scent of iris fades with the light, leaving only the bittersweet.

t_ch: 群星投下异彩，如恋人含情眉眼\n魂灵升腾迷狂，似枯苗饱饮清泉
t_en: The stars cast myriad colors, like the eyes of a lover.\nThe soul ascends in ecstasy, like a withered sapling gulping springwater.

t_ch: 我曾无声呐喊，丛生杂草侵占沃壤良田\n喉舌干涸喑哑，却唤不来野火雹冰闪电
t_en: I was once voiceless to shout, the weeds invading fertile fields.\nThroat and mouth parched and mute, unable to call upon wildfire, hail, and thunder.

t_ch: 而此刻、而眼前，我愿笃信、我将得见——
t_en: And right here and now before me, I sincerely believe that I will see...

t_ch: 隐夜鸫振翅，衔来善的嫩芽与希望的种\n殷勤耕作出，今日的欢歌与明日的笑颜
t_en: The Nightingale beats its wings, carrying with it budding shoots and seeds of hope.\nDiligently toiling the joyful songs of today, and the smiles of tomorrow.

t_ch: 帷幕后晨曦露出边角，将鸟的翎羽点燃\n翼羽携自由逐风追日，将人所应得偿还
t_en: Dawn peeks out from behind the curtain, setting alight the bird's plumes.\nWings of freedom to chase winds and sun, repaying what those deserve.

t_ch: 在此刻、在眼前，我愿笃信、我将得见——\n所向往、所期盼，我愿笃信、我将得见……
t_en: And right here and now before me, I sincerely believe that I shall see...\nThe yearning, the aspiration, I sincerely believe that I shall see...

t_ch: 亲爱的朋友，恭喜你夺得热砂盛典的冠军！
t_en: Dear friend, congrats on winning the Scorchsand Festival championship!

t_ch: 当时的我，也正如现在的你一样——闯过层层关卡，跨越一个又一个障碍，终于在这荒凉的梦境中闯出一片天地。
t_en: I was just like you then — clearing all levels in swathes, leaping and bounding over all obstacles, finally making a name for myself in this desolate Dreamscape.

t_ch: 岁月转瞬而过，我也从一个什么都不懂的毛头小子变成了垂垂老矣的商人。当时，谁又能想到，一个毛头小子竟然会制造出风靡整个梦境的商品呢？
t_en: As time flowed, I went from being an ignorant fledgling to a wizened merchant. Back then, who would even think that a brat would create goods that would take the entire Dreamscape by storm?

t_ch: 咳咳，你瞧，人年纪大了，就喜欢回忆过去…但重要的东西是将来！
t_en: Ahem, don't mind me. The old love to reminisce on the past... What's important is the future!

t_ch: 苏乐达集团的事业不会止步于梦境。我有一个梦想，在不久的将来，哪怕在银河最偏僻的角落都能发现苏乐达的身影，无论贫富贵贱，人人都有畅饮苏乐达的权利！
t_en: The SoulGlad Enterprise won't simply rest their laurels in the Dreamscape. I have a dream, that in the near future, SoulGlad will be found in even the most obscure corners of the Cosmos. Everyone will have the right to enjoy SoulGlad, regardless of status!

t_ch: 为了这样的未来，我会工作至生命的最后一刻。
t_en: I will work to my final breath to realize such a future.

t_ch: 可以聊聊吗？
t_en: Are you up for a chat?

t_ch: 没问题。老人别的没有，多的是闲暇时间，你想聊什么？
t_en: No problem. The old don't have much else besides time. What do you want to talk about?

t_ch: 当时怎么想到创业的？
t_en: What made you start a company back then?

t_ch: 说来话长，我曾是星际和平公司的产品销售，听说匹诺康尼处处是机遇，我便咬紧牙关辞掉了这份人人都羡慕的工作，准备在逐梦时代的浪潮中搏一回。
t_en: That's a long story. I was once an IPC product salesman. Upon hearing that Penacony was flooded with opportunity, I gritted my teeth and resigned from a cushy job that most would die for, ready to bet big in the tide of the Age of Dreaming.

t_ch: 但在梦里赚钱也没那么容易，在一次创业失败后，我只能在热砂的时刻当个普通的搬运工。
t_en: But it's not that easy earning your keep in dreams. After my first business failed, I had to work as an ordinary transport worker in the Moment of Scorchsand.

t_ch: 直到有一天，我因为繁重的工作晕倒在路边，好心人喂给我一瓶美味的饮料，让我找回了活力与梦想…那饮料就是苏乐达！
t_en: Till one day, I fainted by the roadside from overwork. A kind-hearted stranger fed me a tasty beverage that helped me regain my enthusiasm and dreams... And that was SoulGlad!

t_ch: 当我了解了苏乐达的故事，听闻了那位圣人救济罪人的传说，我便大受感动。那时我就立志，一定要将苏乐达和苏萨先生的心愿传向整个宇宙！
t_en: Once I learned about the story behind SoulGlad and the legend of how that saint redeemed the sinners, I was deeply moved. From then on, my resolution was cemented — I was determined to spread SoulGlad and Mr. Sousa's wish to the entire universe!

t_ch: 我将这番愿景讲述给了苜蓿草家系，那位德高望重的「老奥帝」出资赞助我开创了苏乐达工厂。经过千百次口味的调试，无数次的市场考验，才有了今天的苏乐达。
t_en: I told this dream to the Alfalfa Family, and the virtuous Old Oti sponsored the establishment of my SoulGlad factory. It took umpteen taste tests and countless market trials to create the SoulGlad of today.

t_ch: 现在回想起来，是勇气、运气、志气…更重要的是「同谐」的信仰与家族的支持，才让我走上了致富的道路！
t_en: Looking back, it was courage, luck, will... and more importantly, my faith in the Harmony and The Family's support that allowed me to walk the path of fortune!

t_ch: 咳咳，我刚才说的是不是很振奋人心？下次采访我也这么说！
t_en: Ahem, did I move you with my re-telling? I'll do it again the next time you interview me!

t_ch: 苏乐达的由来？
t_en: SoulGlad's origin?

t_ch: 呵呵，没想到现在的年轻人还对这个感兴趣，不枉费我们的大力宣传。那让我讲给你吧——
t_en: Heh-heh, never thought the young'uns now would be interested in such a thing. Our huge advertising push certainly didn't go to waste. I'll tell you about it then...

t_ch: 在匹诺康尼还是监狱之时，有一位叫做「苏萨先生」的大善人。他高超的厨艺本能在宇宙中最豪华的餐厅工作，但却选择来到了边陲监狱中救赎犯人的身体与心灵。
t_en: When Penacony was still a prison, there was a very kind man named Mr. Sousa. He could have worked in the most luxurious restaurant in the universe with his unmatched culinary skill, but he chose to salvage the shells and souls of prisoners in this frontier prison.

t_ch: 为了改善犯人们的生活，苏萨先生靠狱中的食材以及阿斯德纳星系特有的「梦见草」调配出了「苏乐达」。
t_en: To improve the lives of these criminals, Mr. Sousa concocted SoulGlad using ingredients available in the prison and Dreamleaves, an herb that only exists in the Asdana star system.

t_ch: 他希望这种美味的饮品可以让犯人向往美好、化解监狱中的矛盾。这位圣人的梦想便是某日宇宙中的所有人都能坐在一张桌子上，快乐地共饮苏乐达。
t_en: He hoped that this tasty drink would get the prisoners to pine for better days, and quell the uncertainties of imprisonment. This saint's dream was that someday, everyone in the universe would be seated at the same table, happily drinking and sharing SoulGlad.

t_ch: 而狭隘的公司不满苏萨先生纯真的梦想，将他投入狱中折磨，并在依诺哈尔上灭绝了所有的梦乐草，最初的苏乐达从此在宇宙中绝迹。
t_en: But the small-minded IPC was not pleased with Mr. Sousa's sincere dream. They tossed him in prison to be tortured and decimated all the Dreamleaves that were on Enohart. The original SoulGlad would forever be extinct from this universe.

t_ch: 直至战争爆发，不善战斗的苏萨为了实现和平的心愿，与犯人们一同奋战，最终在决战中英勇战死。
t_en: That was till war broke out. Sousa, who was not proficient in combat, stood together with the prisoners and heroically gave his life in the final battle — to realize his wish for peace.

t_ch: 而在将士的战壕中、在家族宴会的桌上、在匹诺康尼的史书里，苏乐达从未缺席……
t_en: And in the trenches of soldiers, at the tables of The Family's banquets, in the history books of Penacony, SoulGlad was ever-present...

t_ch: 而在苏乐达企业与苜蓿草家系的努力下，我们在梦中重新培育了梦见草，让他的梦想在梦中永存——喝一口苏乐达，就是在向这位英雄致敬！
t_en: Through the combined efforts of SoulGlad Enterprises and the Alfalfa Family, we managed to regrow Dreamleaves in dreams. His dream will forever exist in dreams... Every gulp of SoulGlad is a toast to this hero's bravery!

t_ch: 苏乐达的未来规划？
t_en: SoulGlad's future plans?

t_ch: 这个嘛…我们将以苏乐达的饮料业务为核心，向餐饮、潮流服饰、旅游、房地产等多个方面拓展我们的业务。
t_en: Well... With the SoulGlad beverage as our core business, we intend to expand our portfolio into food and beverages, fashion, travel, real estate, and other verticals.

t_ch: 我们会通过各种渠道向银河各界推广苏乐达，力争在每一个重要星球上兴建一座苏乐达主题乐园、一座苏乐达主题公寓、一座苏乐达主题酒店、一座苏乐达服饰广场……
t_en: We will promote SoulGlad across the cosmos using various media channels, heavily investing in key planets to build SoulGlad theme parks, SoulGlad themed apartments, SoulGlad themed hotels, SoulGlad themed fashion plazas...

t_ch: 有什么给年轻人的建议吗？
t_en: Do you have any advice for the younger generation?

t_ch: 现在的年轻人啊，和我们那时候不同，有主见、有思考、有想法，但缺少了信念，做事情常常三心二意，半途而废。
t_en: Young people nowadays, they're different from us back then. They now have opinions, freedom of thought, ideals, but lack resolve. They're usually hesitant when embarking on matters, and give up halfway.

t_ch: 你看我，从来不在乎苏乐达赚了多少钱，我在乎的只是能不能把苏乐达的快乐带给更多的人——这就是同谐的伟大精神。
t_en: Look at me — I've never cared about how much money SoulGlad makes. I only care about whether I can deliver SoulGlad's happiness to even more people. This is the essence of Harmony's spirit.

t_ch: 所以说啊，年轻人不能只想着挣快钱，要沉下心来，努力思考你的信念。重要的不是你赚了多少钱，而是你为什么而工作。
t_en: Which is why, young people shouldn't just think about making a quick buck. They have to find their composure and contemplate on their beliefs. How much you earn isn't important. It is the reason you work.

t_ch: 好棒的建议。
t_en: That's great advice.

t_ch: 多么好的年轻人啊。如果有一天你不想做无名客了，欢迎来苏乐达工作！
t_en: You're an outstanding youngster. You're welcome to work at SoulGlad if the day comes when you don't want to be a Nameless any longer!

t_ch: 建议你不要建议了。
t_en: I advise you not to offer any more advice.

t_ch: 呵呵，这都是我自传里的名句，听我的，准没错。
t_en: Heh-heh, this is an iconic quote from my memoir. You won't go wrong listening to me.

t_ch: 没什么想问的了。
t_en: That's all the questions I have.

t_ch: 不打扰你了。
t_en: I'll leave you to it.

t_ch: 年轻人，努力吧！累了就喝一瓶苏乐达！
t_en: Keep it up, young one! Have a sip of SoulGlad if you're ever tired!

t_ch: 最近苏乐达的销量突然大幅上涨……
t_en: SoulGlad's sales numbers have been rapidly rising recently...

t_ch: 苏乐达集团的银河化战略终于迎来了阶段性的成果！
t_en: SoulGlad Enterprises' cosmic strategy has finally reached a milestone!

t_ch: 多年以来，我们一直坚定不移地将苏乐达推向银河中的各个世界。在这个过程中，因为口味、广告、当地法律等问题，苏乐达的推广一度陷入困境。
t_en: For many years, we have been unwavering in our promotion of SoulGlad to the various worlds in the cosmos. There were many bumps along the journey, over its taste, commercials, local laws, and other problems.

t_ch: 但实践证明，银河化战略是成功的道路，也是必须坚持的策略。
t_en: But the reality has proven that a cosmic strategy is the successful solution, and one in which that we must persist.

t_ch: 自从苏乐达成为匹诺康尼的国民饮料之后，它的销量一直止步不前，这对一个企业来说是十分致命的危险。
t_en: Ever since SoulGlad became the national drink of Penacony, its sales have been climbing higher than ever. This is something that could ironically prove fatal to a company.

t_ch: 现在，许多世界已经开始慢慢接受苏乐达，苏乐达集团的销售额即将到达新的高峰！
t_en: Now, many worlds have slowly begun to accept SoulGlad. SoulGlad Enterprise's sales numbers are about to reach a new high!

t_ch: 集团发展至今，人员冗余，流程复杂，是时候该改革了……
t_en: With the Enterprise' expansion till today, some staff will be made redundant, and with overly complex work flows, it is about time for a restructuring...

t_ch: 大集团有大集团的好处，也有大集团特有的毛病。
t_en: Major groups have their advantages, as well as problems only unique to them.

t_ch: 还记得我刚刚开办苏乐达工厂的时候，任何事情都能当天批示，当天决定，当天推进。
t_en: I remember when I just opened my SoulGlad factory — anything could be submitted, approved, and rolled out on the same day.

t_ch: 现在集团大则大矣，一件小事都要经过无数领导的批示，想要做些创新简直难如登天。这样下去，我们很快会丧失竞争优势。
t_en: The current company is now a juggernaut. Every minute detail has to be scrutinized by countless managers. Trying to implement some innovation is as hard as getting into heaven. At this rate, we'll very quickly lose our competitive edge.

t_ch: 该和集团的人力部门商谈该如何降本增效了，首先，就要开除那些不做事的员工！
t_en: It's time to have a chat with HR on measures of cost reduction and higher efficiency. First on the list is to dismiss the employees with subpar performances!

t_ch: 年轻人，你觉得呢？
t_en: Young one, what do you think?

t_ch: 完全支持！
t_en: Two thumbs up!

t_ch: 哈哈哈，我们就是需要像你一样高觉悟的员工！
t_en: Hahaha, we need more insightful workers just like you!

t_ch: 裁员会给赔偿吗？
t_en: Will retrenched staff get severance pay?

t_ch: 赔偿？不让他们赔偿公司的损失已经够人道了！不过作为苜蓿草旗下品牌，我们当然也有「同谐」的人情味——
t_en: Severance? Not making them pay for the company's losses is generous enough! But seeing as we need to be on-brand with the Alfalfa Family, we must also express compassion that's aligned with the Harmony...

t_ch: 被开除的员工可以获得三个月的月薪…等价的艾迪恩代币，能肯定能供他们玩到有新工作为止。
t_en: Dismissed staff will get three months' pay... in Aideen tokens. That will be more than enough to entertain them till they find new jobs.

t_ch: 感觉没什么用。
t_en: Doesn't sound it's of much help.

t_ch: 那就全部换一批人，反正不缺找工作的人…有壮士断腕决心，才有成功的可能。
t_en: Then I'll replace all of them at once. Everyone's hankering for a job these days anyway... Success is only possible with a will that has learned loss.

t_ch: 可恶，法院竟然没有判定「苏萨水」侵权！
t_en: Bah, the court did not rule Sousa Juice guilty with copyright infringement!

t_ch: 集团的法务部都是干什么吃的，竟然连这么简单的案件都打不赢！回去之后，我一定要好好地优化他们！
t_en: Is the Enterprise's Justice Department incompetent? They can't even win such a simple case! When I return, I'm definitely going to take my time optimizing them!

t_ch: 相似的包装、相似的口味、相似的广告词…法官如果不是盲人，就一定能看出来苏萨水就是个彻头彻尾的抄袭品牌！
t_en: Similar packaging, similar taste, similar tagline... Unless the judge is blind, it's so obvious Sousa Juice is a <unbreak>100%</unbreak> copycat of our brand!

t_ch: 还说什么苏乐达其实是抄袭苏萨水，他们才是苏萨先生作品的正统继承者，完全是无稽之谈！
t_en: They still have the audacity to claim SoulGlad as copied Sousa Juice, and that they're the true successor of Mr. Sousa's work. Absolute and total hogwash!

t_ch: 说的不对吗？
t_en: Are they wrong?

t_ch: 真是太可恶了！
t_en: This is preposterous!

t_ch: 哼，苏乐达一直被模仿，但从未被超越。销量将会证明一切！
t_en: Hmph, SoulGlad has always had its fair share of copycats, but none has surpassed us. Our sales will prove it once and for all!

t_ch: 我还能看到苏乐达流淌在银河各处那一天吗？
t_en: Will I see the day when SoulGlad dominates every corner of the cosmos?

t_ch: 岁月不饶人啊，虽然我身子骨还硬朗，记性也是一天比一天差。
t_en: Time is a merciless mistress. My bones may still be sturdy, but my memory gets worse with each day.

t_ch: 最近我老是想不起一些东西的名字，手上拿着手机，却在找手机在哪……
t_en: I haven't been able to remember the names of some things recently. Or I've got my phone in my hand, but wonder where it is...

t_ch: 唉，医生告诉我，人的记忆功能退化的时候，会先忘记名词，再忘记动词，因为动词比名词重要些……
t_en: *sigh* The doctors tell me that when a person's memory starts fading, he will first forget nouns, then verbs, because verbs are more important than nouns...

t_ch: 苏乐达集团正处在关键的发展节点，我们还没有成为宇宙中最赚钱的汽水品牌…也没有实现苏萨先生的理想呢！
t_en: The SoulGlad Enterprise is at a pivotal development phase. We still haven't become the richest soda brand in the universe... nor have we fulfilled Mr. Sousa's ideal!

t_ch: 拜托，命运，让我发挥完余热之后再熄灭吧……
t_en: I would plead with destiny to allow me to shine bright with whatever I have left before I am extinguished...

t_ch: 哦~这位生面孔的顾客，你是来照顾我的生意？还是想跟我畅聊一番？
t_en: Ooh, a familiar face. Are you here to take care of my business? Or for a chat?

t_ch: 你是谁？
t_en: Who are you?

t_ch: 哎呀~好问题！是个很有深度的问题！
t_en: Ooh good question! A very profound one!

t_ch: 对于匹诺康尼而言，我是一名无籍的「偷渡客」；对于我的母星而言，我是「LOV-E三代量产型<unbreak>3451</unbreak>」；对流梦礁的居民而言，我叫「基尔克」。
t_en: To Penacony, I am a stateless stowaway. To my home planet, I am LOV-E III Mass Production Model <unbreak>3451</unbreak>. To the residents of Dreamflux Reef, I am Kirk.

t_ch: 对你来说，可以把我当作是一个「玩具商人」，喜欢用些小玩意儿唤醒无机生命的快感回路，刺激有机生命的伏隔核~
t_en: As for you, you can see me as a toy merchant who has a penchant for using knick-knacks to jolt the dopamine circuits of inorganic creatures, and to tickle the thrills of the nucleus accumbens of organic life!

t_ch: 哦~顾客，你喜欢玩具吗？
t_en: Ohh, do you like toys?

t_ch: 喜欢。
t_en: I do.

t_ch: 哦！居然还能遇到喜欢玩具的成熟体有机生命，真是令人欣喜！
t_en: Oh! To have the fortune to meet a fully grown organic lifeform who likes toys. What a delight!

t_ch: 得看是什么玩具了。
t_en: I'll have to see what toy it is.

t_ch: 哦~那这些精心设计的小玩具定不会令你失望呀~
t_en: Ohh, then these exquisitely designed toys won't let you down!

t_ch: 现在谁还玩玩具啊。
t_en: Who still plays with toys in this day and age?

t_ch: 哦，我的博识尊，你残酷的语言让我的心脏回路发生了一次小短路！
t_en: Oh, my Learned Nous, your cruel words have caused a short circuit in my heart circuit!

t_ch: 但没关系，你说得对，很少有有机生命爱玩玩具。
t_en: But it's okay. You're right — few organic lifeforms like toys.

t_ch: 现在的宇宙中有太多高效取悦你们的娱乐方式了，电子软件、虚拟现实、化合物质……在匹诺康尼更是如此，梦境中有千万种玩乐的方式，何必把弄一个小玩具。
t_en: There are plenty more entertaining ways that can elicit joy more efficiently in the universe: Software, virtual reality, chemical substances... Even more so on Penacony, where there are myriad methods of entertainment in the Dreamscape. Why even bother with an insignificant toy?

t_ch: 但对于一些无机生命来说，玩具这种有趣的「装置」往往比拟造规则的游戏和电子脑中的虚构感官刺激要有吸引力。
t_en: But to some inorganic beings, toys are fun devices that are more mesmerizing than games with artificial rules and virtual sensory stimulation in electronic brains.

t_ch: 不是每位智械都有螺丝星臣民那样精妙的结构，我们的很多族群曾经也只是有机生命的「玩具」。所以在我们眼中，探索玩具也是在把玩与自己同源的一具躯体……
t_en: Not every Intellitron has the structural intricacy of Planet Screwllum's citizens. Many of us were once "toys" to organic lifeforms. In our eyes, tinkering with toys is also playing with a framework that has the same origin as us...

t_ch: 听起来很有意思。
t_en: Sounds very interesting.

t_ch: 对吧，用这样的说法果然能够让你们有机生命更好理解。
t_en: I know, right? I was quite certain organic beings such as you would understand the concept more easily if explained this way.

t_ch: 听起来有点恶心。
t_en: Sound a little disgusting.

t_ch: 是吗？我以为自己只是用了一种便于有机生命理解的话术…看来我还是没有完全理解你们的礼仪，抱歉。
t_en: Really? I thought I was using vernacular apposite to organic beings... It appears I don't fully understand your customs. My apologies.

t_ch: 玩这么大？
t_en: Are you serious?

t_ch: 哦~恕我冒犯，但宇宙中在追寻愉悦上走得最远、最疯狂、最刺激的永远是你们有机生命。
t_en: Oh! I apologize for any offense, but when it comes to the pursuit of joy, organic beings like you will always go the farthest, the wildest, and the most thrilling route in the universe.

t_ch: 我爱这里——在这片梦境里，无论是有机生命还是无机生命，大家的情感可以自由地宣泄，我也希望自己的产品能让这种宣泄更进一步……
t_en: I love this place. In this Dreamscape, be it an organic or inorganic lifeform, everyone's emotions can be expressed freely. I also hope that my own products can elevate such forms of expression...

t_ch: 顾客，有机会就来看看我的生意吧——我的玩具可不是普通的玩具，保准让您满意。
t_en: Come take a look at my business when you get a chance — my toys are not your common fare. I guarantee your satisfaction.

t_ch: 可以聊聊吗？
t_en: Are you up for a chat?

t_ch: 哦~没问题！请尽情向我灌注你的情感吧，可爱的有机生命！
t_en: Ohh no problem! Please project your emotions on to me to your heart's desire, lovely organic being!

t_ch: 你都卖些什么玩意儿？
t_en: What do you sell?

t_ch: 玩具，我的顾客，有趣的、精巧的、神奇的，能为你带来愉悦感的玩具。
t_en: Toys, dear customer. Interesting, intricate, and wondrous toys that can bequeath you with joy.

t_ch: 看这魔方大师设计的七色六面魔方，如同宇宙本身一样玄妙莫测；看这用情绪碎片铸造的奇妙积木，能诱发你内心深处最真实的情感……
t_en: This colorful magic cube was designed by the cubemaster, as unfathomable as the universe itself. Just look at this wondrous building block that was crafted from emotion fragments — it will reveal the truest emotions in the deepest crevices of your heart...

t_ch: 哦~怎么样？买回家试试看吧？
t_en: Ohh, how about it? Buy one home and try it out?

t_ch: 你怎么来流梦礁的？
t_en: How did you come to the Dreamflux Reef?

t_ch: 哦~这就说来话长了。
t_en: Ohh, now that's a long story.

t_ch: 我的生产地是阿斯蒙星环，那地方在追逐「欢愉」中走了条偏路，所有人都寻求疯狂的感官刺激，以此渴求乐子神的瞥视。
t_en: I was manufactured in the Asmon Rim, a planet that took a wrong turn in their pursuit of the Elation. Everyone sought heightened stimulation of the senses, all craving for a mere glimpse from the Laughter.

t_ch: 他们在这条路上走得越来越极端，最后摧毁了道德，把其他生命都当成了娱乐的玩具——我也是玩具之一。
t_en: The path they took became more extreme, finally destroying any remaining vestiges of their morality and treated other lives as toys of entertainment — I was one of those toys.

t_ch: 但说真的，我理解不了他们寻求快乐的方式，作为玩具，我一点也不快乐！于是我逃出了那里，乘着一位喜欢我的顾客的船来到了匹诺康尼。
t_en: But to be honest, I could never understand the method in which they sought joy. As a toy, I was not happy at all! That's why I escaped, boarded a ship of a customer who favored me, and made my way to Penacony.

t_ch: 我在蓝调的时刻的舞厅找了份工作，直到…一只好酷好酷的大怪物撕裂了我——天呐，那真是我被生产以来体验到的最刺激的事情！
t_en: I found a job at a dance hall in the Blue Hour, until... A super cool monster ripped me to shreds. Gosh, that really was the most thrilling incident I had experienced since I was made!

t_ch: 等到我的系统恢复，就已经在流梦礁了。比起上面，我更喜欢这个地方，没有家族盯着，能够更自由地感受大家激烈的情绪，哦~
t_en: When my system recovered, I was already at the Dreamflux Reef. I much prefer this place to the higher layers. Without The Family's eyes on me, I can freely experience everyone's intense emotions, ohh!

t_ch: 没什么想问的了。
t_en: That's all the questions I have.

t_ch: 我想看看你的玩具。
t_en: I wish to take a look at your toys.

t_ch: 看吧，我的顾客，想怎么看就怎么看~
t_en: Look away, customer. Look at them any way you want!

t_ch: 下次见。
t_en: I'll see you next time.

t_ch: 哦~亲爱的顾客，要记得再来哦~
t_en: Ohh my dear customer, please come back again!

t_ch: 哦吼吼吼~我帮他们找到了自己，真是太好啦！
t_en: Oh-hohoho, I helped them find themselves. That's wonderful!

t_ch: 哦~顾客，我好开心，好开心呀！
t_en: Ohh customer, I'm delighted, so delighted!

t_ch: 您知道吗，我刚刚听说为宾客服务的梦境迷钟们开始失控了——您知道这是谁的功劳吗？
t_en: Did you know — I just heard that the Dream Tickers servicing the guests have started to malfunction. Do you know who's the genius behind this?

t_ch: 是我，基尔克！
t_en: It's me, Kirk!

t_ch: 我的积木是用无比激烈的情绪碎片制成的，能帮人激发出被压抑的欲望，这种效果在他们这些梦境造物身上会更加明显……
t_en: My building block was made from exceptionally intense emotion fragments that can help people evoke desires that have been suppressed. This effect will be much more evident on these Dreamscape creations...

t_ch: 太好了，他们用我的玩具找到了自我，真是让人开心呀~
t_en: Excellent. They found themselves using my toys. I'm delighted!

t_ch: 你真是好人。
t_en: You're such a good person.

t_ch: 哎呀，夸得我都不好意思啦！
t_en: Oh dear, I'm all ablush from your praise!

t_ch: 你让他们坏掉了。
t_en: You broke them.

t_ch: 哦~那不是坏掉，那是他们找到了自我，不再是奴隶的表现~
t_en: Ohh, not broken. They've found their own selves, and aren't slaves anymore!

t_ch: 不管别人怎么说，我还会继续制造更加激烈的玩具，让更多人能自由宣泄自己的情感，这就是玩具的意义呀~
t_en: No matter what others say, I will continue to craft even intenser toys that will let even more people bare their emotions. This is the essence of toys!

t_ch: 有机生命的快乐与无机生命的快乐，究竟是不是同一种快乐呢……
t_en: The joys of organic and inorganic beings... Are they the same type of joy...?

t_ch: 虽然无机生命能感受有机生命的情绪、能从生物学上理解有机生命取悦自己的方式，但我们能真正理解与自己构造完全不同的物种是何等感受吗？
t_en: Inorganic life can feel the emotions of organic life, and can understand how they derive pleasure from a biological standpoint, but can we truly understand the sort of emotions felt by a species whose structural make is entirely different from ours?

t_ch: 亲爱的顾客，你觉得呢——我能完全理解你的快乐，并为你带来快乐吗？
t_en: My dear customer, what do you think? Did I completely understand your joy, and evoke joy in you?

t_ch: 能。
t_en: You did.

t_ch: 哎，希望如此~
t_en: *sigh* I sure hope so!

t_ch: 不能。
t_en: No.

t_ch: 哦，真是可惜~
t_en: Oh, such a pity!

t_ch: 你试试？
t_en: Why don't you give it a go?

t_ch: 哎呀~你真调皮~
t_en: Ooh, you're so naughty!

t_ch: 虽然螺丝咕姆那样伟大的同胞提出了很多理论来证明我们的相近，但从诞生起就在取悦人类的我无法认同那些理论。
t_en: Our great comrade Screwllum may have posited many theories to prove our similarities, but I, who was made to provide joy to humans, do not accede to those theories.

t_ch: 即便我们的起源与人类一样来自宇宙的演变，但比起神秘莫测的有机生命，我们却像一张白纸，从构造模式到思维系统，没有一点秘密可言……
t_en: Even if our origin is the result of the universe's evolution, just like humanity, we are like a blank piece of paper compared to the profundity of organic life — from our structural modes to thought systems, there are no secrets to speak of...

t_ch: 同样的快感，人类与生俱来就能感受到，但我们必须通过感官硬件与仿生程序才能触发……
t_en: Humans can feel pleasure the moment they are born, but we have to trigger it through sensory hardware and bionic programming...

t_ch: 但这绝不意味着智械比有机生命更低级，我们也可以拥有自己独特的快乐。我的玩具只是第一步，未来，我会为同胞创造出更独一无二的快乐之物。
t_en: But this doesn't mean that Intellitrons are inferior to organic lives. We also possess our own unique joys. My toys are just the first step — in the future, I will create an item of joy exclusively for my brethren.

t_ch: 哎呀！「美梦剧团」们真是太可怜了，我看不下去了！
t_en: Oh dear! The Sweet Dreams Troupe members are such poor things. I can't bear this any longer!

t_ch: 哦~看看那些失去自由的孩子，在宣扬快乐的梦境里，却没有资格享受自己的快乐…我一定要帮帮他们！
t_en: Ohh, look at those children who have lost their freedom. In a Dreamscape that promotes happiness, yet unworthy to enjoy their own... I must help them!

t_ch: 顾客，你一定在梦里见过那些痛苦的「美梦剧团」吧？
t_en: Customer, you must have seen those suffering Sweet Dreams Troupe members in the Dreamscape, no?

t_ch: 是的，我见过很多。
t_en: Yes, many of them.

t_ch: 哎呀！是吧，那么多有情感的小家伙在梦中挣扎，真是让人心疼！
t_en: Oh no! These poor souls, full of emotion, struggling in a dream. It's utterly heart-breaking!

t_ch: 他们不痛苦吧。
t_en: I'm sure they aren't suffering.

t_ch: 天呐！您的话语比我的金属外壳还冰冷，他们怎能不痛苦呢？
t_en: Heavens! Your words are colder than my metal chassis. How could they not be suffering?

t_ch: 惊梦剧团倒是见过不少。
t_en: I've seen plenty of the Dreamjolt Troupe though.

t_ch: 哎呀！那就是他们在痛苦中无路可走，最后爆发出来的样子！
t_en: Oh no! That's the result of them having nowhere to turn to in their constant suffering!

t_ch: 那些可怜的孩子，从诞生起就必须遵从取悦宾客的使命，无法探寻自己的欲望与情感，让我想到了过去的自己……
t_en: From the time they are made, those poor children have been instructed to entertain their guests, unable to explore their own desires and emotions. They remind me of my former self...

t_ch: 家族宣称他们不是有机生命，只是用梦境材料与情感构造的工具——开什么玩笑，他们有身体，有灵魂，有欲望，这和我们无机生命有什么两样！
t_en: The Family proclaims that they are not organic lifeforms, but tools constructed from the Dreamscape materials and emotions — Preposterous! They have bodies, souls, and desires! How is that any different from us inorganic beings!

t_ch: 我会制造更多让他们找到自我的玩具，让这些朋友能自由选择快乐的方式，我一定会做到的！
t_en: I will make even more toys to help them find themselves, to help these friends choose the path of joy of their own free will, I swear!

t_ch: 唉，生意越来越差了，要不要改行呢……
t_en: *sigh* Business is getting worse. Should I change occupations...

t_ch: 亲爱的顾客，玩具产业已经锈迹斑斑了，在这座破工厂坍塌之前，或许我该及时撤离……
t_en: Dear customer, the toy industry is already reaching its twilight. Perhaps I should evacuate this rundown factory before it collapses...

t_ch: 宇宙中最大的玩具厂前一阵刚发布宣告，声称自己被星际和平公司收购后会将业务转向军工行业，将他们的玩具机器人用于「存护」的正义战争。
t_en: The universe's biggest toy vendor recently announced that it would pivot its efforts towards military equipment after being acquired by the IPC, using their toy robots to help the righteous war fought by "Preservation."

t_ch: 在我曾经是「玩具」的那段时光里，阿斯蒙人不允许我们体验他们的快乐，但又为了乐趣给我们加装了丰富的情感模块，名为「无聊」的病毒无时无刻不在侵蚀我的系统。
t_en: During my time as a toy, the Asmonians never allowed us to experience their joy, yet installed a repertoire of emotional modules for their own entertainment, resulting in the virus called "boredom" that constantly pervaded my system.

t_ch: 我没有崩溃的原因，是一位客人留下的积木玩具。
t_en: The reason I didn't crash was all thanks to a building block toy left behind by a guest.

t_ch: 那积木只有三块，连一座塔都搭不起来，但那三块积木是我最好的朋友，是我的救星……
t_en: There are only three of such building blocks. Even stacked, they can't make a tower, but those three blocks are my best friends, and my salvation...

t_ch: 来到匹诺康尼之后，我可以自由地做各种想做的事情，但我再也找不回跟那三块积木相伴时的满足了。
t_en: Upon arriving on Penacony, I could do anything as I wished, but I could not re-discover that sense of satisfaction that I experienced with those three blocks.

t_ch: 对你们也一样吧，有了丰富的物质世界，这样单纯简陋的玩乐毫无意义，对吗？
t_en: It's probably the same for you: In a material world full of wonder, such plain, shoddy distractions are meaningless, no?

t_ch: 基尔克沉默了，他之前的热情奔放荡然无存。即使他的外壳无法看出表情，你也能在与你完全不同的钢铁生命之上，感受到一种让你为之共鸣的悲伤。
t_en: Kirk goes silent, his prior passionate enthusiasm evidently absent. Even if his chassis betrays no change in emotion, you can feel a sense of sorrow that resonates with you from that life wrapped in steel that is a stark contrast to your fleshy self.

t_ch: 你好，无名客。
t_en: Hello, Nameless.

t_ch: 欢迎来到流梦礁。如你所见，这才是梦境原本的样子…没有声色犬马的狂欢和派对，这里有的只是一团混沌的忆质、还有一群在噩梦中努力抗争的人们。
t_en: Welcome to the Dreamflux Reef. As you can see, this is the Dreamscape's original form... No wild celebrations nor parties, just a bundle of chaotic memoria, and a resistance force battling ardently within nightmares.

t_ch: 欢迎你加入「钟表匠」的事业，{NICKNAME}。我很荣幸——假如米哈伊尔还在，他一定也会这么觉得。
t_en: You're welcome to join the Watchmaker's cause, {NICKNAME}. I'd be honored. If Mikhail were still around, I'm sure he would feel the same.

t_ch: 你以前也是无名客吗？
t_en: Were you also a Nameless once?

t_ch: 我和很多无名客有过接触，甚至和他们成为了朋友…但不，我不是他们中的一员。
t_en: I've encountered many Nameless, even becoming friends with them... But no, I'm not one of them.

t_ch: 在这里生活一定很不容易。
t_en: Life here must be hard.

t_ch: 恐怕在这世上本就没有什么易事。但这也彰显了人的强韧，我们总能找到办法适应环境，然后生存下来。
t_en: I'm afraid nothing in this world is easy. But that just builds character — we'll always find a way to adapt to our surroundings, and live on in turn.

t_ch: 从少年时代起，我就一直幻想着能登上星穹列车，成为「开拓」的践行者。
t_en: Since my adolescent days, I've always dreamed of boarding the Astral Express and becoming an advocate of the Trailblaze.

t_ch: 米哈伊尔给我讲了很多旅行奇遇；拉扎莉娜教会了我如何查阅星图；铁尔南教会了我该怎么拿起武器，保护自己。
t_en: Mikhail told me of his many adventures. Razalina taught me how to read the astral charts. Tiernan taught me how to use weapons to protect myself.

t_ch: 通过和他们对话，我逐渐在脑海里拼凑出了星海的全貌，哪怕我打出生起就从没离开过阿斯德纳。
t_en: Through conversations with them, my mind gradually pieced together the entire cartography of the stars, even if I had never left Asdana since the day I was born.

t_ch: 你现在还想出去看看吗？
t_en: Do you still wish to take a look outside?

t_ch: 没有一刻不想。
t_en: There's never been a moment where I haven't.

t_ch: 听上去有些遗憾。
t_en: I sense a tinge of regret in your words.

t_ch: 是莫大的遗憾。
t_en: A great regret.

t_ch: 但我已经不会感到沮丧了。相反，我目睹了一些花朵、一些美好的东西从遗憾的裂缝里生长了出来。
t_en: But I'm not depressed anymore. In contrast, from the crevices of regret, I've seen some flowers and other beautiful things grow.

t_ch: 我已经找到了自己的使命：守护这片梦域，引导所有误入其中的人们寻回自我。对现在的我来说，就算是注定无法实现的理想也可以成为动力，驱使我认真地生活、前进。
t_en: I've found my own calling: Protect this dream, and guide those who've stumbled into it to find themselves again. For the present me, even if I'm destined not to realize my ideals, it can still become the motivation to drive myself to live life earnestly, and to move forward.

t_ch: 「注定无法实现」的意思是…
t_en: "Destined not to realize my ideals," that means...

t_ch: 如果用现实中的时间来计数…我已经很老了，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: If we were to calculate using real-time... I'm already very old, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 我的肉身恐怕早已磨灭。现在呈现在你面前的这个形象，大概和很久以前刚刚步入梦境的那个米凯无异。
t_en: I'm afraid my flesh is already long gone. The image I portray before you now is probably no different from the Micah who first entered the Dreamscape.

t_ch: 我一直在这片漫无尽头的梦境中守候着，等着有人能够响应「钟表匠」的号召，继承他的理想。
t_en: I've been standing guard in this endless Dreamscape, waiting for someone to return the Watchmaker's echo, to inherit his wish.

t_ch: 见到你们以后，我感觉格外…平静。相比我那无法实现的理想，你们的到来更像是一个早晚都会兑现的承诺。
t_en: After meeting you all, I felt especially... peaceful. Compared to that unfulfilled ideal of mine, your arrival is more akin to a promise that will eventually be realized.

t_ch: 就像他以前说过的那样：「希望和理想不会消失，只是一时迷了路。挺过去！它们一定能找到回家的办法。」
t_en: It is just as he said before: "Hope and dreams will not vanish. They'll just be lost momentarily. Bear with it! They will surely find their way home."

t_ch: 你好，无名客。希望你还习惯在流梦礁的生活。
t_en: Hello, Nameless. I hope life at the Dreamflux Reef hasn't been too jarring for you.

t_ch: 如果遇到任何困难就来找我吧，我会尽己所能提供一切帮助。
t_en: Seek me out if you run into any difficulties. I will provide help any way I can.

t_ch: 米凯，我想和你聊聊天。
t_en: Micah, I want to chat with you.

t_ch: 随时随地，朋友。
t_en: Anytime, anywhere, friend.

t_ch: 讲讲你自己的经历吧。
t_en: Tell me of your experiences.

t_ch: 我出生在匹诺康尼的监狱星时期，我的生父生母都是曾在阿斯德纳开采忆质的星际囚犯的后人。
t_en: I was born when Penacony was still a prison. My biological parents were once descendants of interstellar criminals during Asdana's memoria harvesting period.

t_ch: 有关他们的记忆已经有些模糊了——在那场反抗压迫的战争中，无数和我一样的孩子失去了亲人、朋友。
t_en: My memories of them are hazy. In that war against our oppressors, many children like me lost our kin and friends.

t_ch: 我很幸运，因为我遇到了米哈伊尔。他从硝烟弹雨的战场上将我救下，又把我当作自己的孩子一般抚养。对我来说，他就是真正的「父亲」。
t_en: I was lucky, because I met Mikhail. He rescued me from the smoke and ashes of war, and raised me as one of his own. To me, he was my real father.

t_ch: 米哈伊尔，拉扎莉娜，铁尔南…我在他们的庇护下长大成人，当上了战机飞行员，加入了解放阿斯德纳的战斗。我和他们并肩作战，一起流下象征解放的血与泪。
t_en: Mikhail, Razalina, Tiernan... I grew up under their protection, became a combat pilot, and joined the fight to liberate Asdana. I fought alongside them, shedding blood and tears that symbolized liberty.

t_ch: 他决定离开匹诺康尼、奔向星际拓荒前线的那天，米哈伊尔把梦境世界托付给了我。但他离开了太久…实在太久。匹诺康尼又一次陷入了混乱，局面超出了我的掌控。
t_en: The day he decided to leave Penacony and head for the interstellar frontier, Mikhail entrusted the Dreamscape to me. But he left for too long... far too long. Penacony was once again plunged into chaos, with the ensuing situation spun beyond my control.

t_ch: 后面的故事你应该已经有所了解了。我的一生都在追随父亲的脚步，他的理想亦是我的。即便他已经离开，「钟表匠」的传说也会一直在匹诺康尼流传下去。
t_en: I'm sure you know what happened after. All my life, I followed in my father's footsteps — his dream was mine. He may be gone, but the Watchmaker's legend has always been passed down on Penacony.

t_ch: 我想再多了解一下流梦礁。
t_en: I wish to learn more about the Dreamflux Reef.

t_ch: 无名客帮助阿斯德纳的原住民们建立起了最初的梦境城市，一个仅存于忆质中的完美避难所。
t_en: The Nameless helped the indigenous Asdanans establish the first Dreamscape city, a perfect shelter existing within memoria.

t_ch: 但在米哈伊尔离开的那段时间里，匹诺康尼各大家系之间的内乱让梦境世界遭受了毁灭性的破坏。
t_en: But during the period when Mikhail left, civil strife broke out among Penacony's Families, causing much destructive damage to the Dreamscape.

t_ch: 家族建立起盛会之星后，混乱的原初梦境再也无人问津。直到家族人畜无害的面具被米哈伊尔撕碎，才有极少数人意识到那片浮华表象之下潜藏的歪曲动机。
t_en: After The Family established the Planet of Festivities, the chaotic Primordial Dreamscape was left unchecked. It wasn't until Mikhail ripped off The Family's faux-benevolent mask, that just a handful of people realized that sinister motives lurked beneath that ostentatious representation.

t_ch: 在那之后，加拉赫用「神秘」的力量重新激活了这片不受家族掌控的梦境飞地——这就是流梦礁的来历。
t_en: After that, Gallagher used Enigmata's power to once again activate this soaring Dreamscape unfettered by The Family's shackles — this is the origin of Dreamflux Reef.

t_ch: 以流梦礁为据点，我和加拉赫尽己所能，引导来到这里的人们发觉藏于家族美梦之后的真相。
t_en: With the Dreamflux Reef as our stronghold, Gallagher and I gave everything to guide the people who came here to realize the truth behind The Family's dream.

t_ch: 但我们不会强迫任何人留下。我们只希望能吸引到志同道合的战友，加入这场对抗虚假美梦的抗争。
t_en: But we never forced anyone to stay. We only hoped to gather enough like-minded comrades to join in the resistance against this false dream.

t_ch: 能说说你的父亲吗？
t_en: Can you talk about your father?

t_ch: 米哈伊尔…我的父亲并不完美。但他的一生都在燃烧，发亮。
t_en: Mikhail... My father was not perfect, but he burned bright his entire life.

t_ch: 他永远怀揣理想，即使是在最黑暗的日子里也从没放弃过希望。他有太多没能做到的事…但他从未因惧怕失败放慢追逐自由的步伐，或犹豫是否该向求救之人伸出援手。
t_en: His ideals were always close to his heart. He never lost hope, even on the darkest days. He had too many things he could not do... but that never stopped his pursuit of freedom, not from fear of failure. Neither did he hesitate to lend a helping hand to those in need.

t_ch: 我没有见过他口中那位伟大的阿基维利。但在我心中，拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔就是「开拓」的符号。
t_en: I've never seen that great Akivili that he spoke of. But in my heart, Mikhail Char Legwork was a symbol of the Trailblaze.

t_ch: 暂时只想说这些。
t_en: I would like to stop here.

t_ch: 祝你在梦中平安，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: May you be safe in your dreams, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 不打扰你了。
t_en: I'll leave you to it.

t_ch: 祝你在梦中平安，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: May you be safe in your dreams, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 你尝试用钟表把戏触碰米凯的内心……
t_en: You try to use Clockwork to touch Micah's heart...

t_ch: …但他的心绪表盘没有在你面前显现。
t_en: ...But his Emo Dial does not appear before you.

t_ch: 站在你眼前的是一个坚定的人，一个无需依靠他人来引导其心绪走向的人。
t_en: Standing before you is a resolute individual, one who does not rely on others to guide his feelings.

t_ch: 哪怕将全部的人生都献给了一片支离破碎的梦域，他的精神依旧完整，不接受丝毫的动摇。
t_en: Even if everyone's lives were offered to this shattered dream, his spirit would remain intact, unwavering even in the slightest.

t_ch: …陌生的面孔？
t_en: ...A stranger's visage?

t_ch: 既然你造访了流梦礁，还能走到这里来，说明你对这场美梦已经很了解了。
t_en: That you visited the Dreamflux Reef and still made it this far, it means you know this dream very well.

t_ch: 我问你一个问题——知道「哈努努」这个名字吗？
t_en: Let me ask you a question: Do you know the name "Hanunue"?

t_ch: 我知道。
t_en: I know.

t_ch: 不错，不过——听过他名字的人很多，了解他的人却很少。
t_en: That's right, but while many have heard his name, few truly know him.

t_ch: 不知道。
t_en: I don't know.

t_ch: 呵，没关系，这片该死的梦就是这样，总会把英雄的名字埋在夜里。
t_en: Heh, no matter. It's this cursed dream that buries the names of heroes in the night.

t_ch: 我只认识「哈努兄弟」。
t_en: I only know Brother Hanu.

t_ch: 唉，「钟表匠」先生创作它本是想铭记他的故事，如今却反倒将他的存在掩盖了。
t_en: *sigh* Mr. Watchmaker initially wanted to make the cartoon to preserve his story, but the cartoon ironically buried his actual existence.

t_ch: 哈努努，来自纳肖巴的哈贝克族星际通缉犯，于琥珀历<unbreak>2147</unbreak>纪一手策办了蒙托尔星系的大动乱，被星际和平公司判处终身监禁，于阿斯德纳边陲监狱服刑。
t_en: Hanunue was a wanted interstellar criminal hailing from the Harbeck clan on Nashoba. In <unbreak>2147</unbreak> AE, he single-handedly masterminded the tumult in the Montour System. He was finally apprehended and sentenced by the IPC, and served his time in Asdana's frontier prison.

t_ch: 但这个罪犯入狱后并不安分，还没待上几年，就又策划了一场闻名星海的暴动…致使一个陌生的名字出现在了阿斯德纳星系——你一定知道这个名字。
t_en: But this criminal was not repentant during his incarceration. Within just a few years, he plotted another riot that is well-known among the stars... giving rise to an unfamiliar name appearing in the Asdana star system — you must know of this name.

t_ch: 是匹诺康尼。
t_en: It is Penacony.

t_ch: 嗯，就是这里，盛会之星——匹诺康尼。
t_en: Yes, this very planet, the Planet of Festivities — Penacony.

t_ch: 是哈努星。
t_en: It's the planet of Hanu.

t_ch: 哼，那位英雄没有那么高调。他将自己亲手解放的星球命名——匹诺康尼。
t_en: Hmph, that hero was not so ostentatious. He named the planet that he personally liberated — Penacony.

t_ch: 是球棒星。
t_en: It's Planet Baseball Bat.

t_ch: 呃，那位英雄不爱打棒球，只喜欢打架。他将自己亲手解放的星球命名——匹诺康尼。
t_en: Err, that hero much preferred brawling to baseball. He named the planet that he personally liberated — Penacony.

t_ch: 多么伟大的英雄，却在自己的领土上无人问津，甚至被后人解读为无谋的暴君，何其可悲！
t_en: Such a great hero, yet unsung in his own territory. He was even labeled a witless tyrant by later generations. The tragedy!

t_ch: 所以…我请求「钟表匠」将书写《小小哈努兄弟》故事的任务交给了我，不知道你有没有玩过这个游戏。
t_en: So... I asked the Watchmaker to entrust me with the task of writing the Lil' Brother Hanu. I don't know whether you've played that game before.

t_ch: 狗策划，原来是你！
t_en: So you were that cursed game designer!

t_ch: 快增加投放奖励！
t_en: You need to add more rewards!

t_ch: 快降低关卡难度！
t_en: You need to reduce the stage difficulty!

t_ch: 呃…游戏是克劳克影业做的，我只负责写故事书。
t_en: Err... The game was made by Clock Studios, and I was only in charge of writing the narrative.

t_ch: 抱歉，我不怎么会隐瞒，只能把那个老人告诉我的事情原封不动地夹在了故事中。
t_en: Sorry, I'm not great at embellishing things. I just retold the story that the old man told me verbatim.

t_ch: 如果你能从中了解到哈努努的故事，那就太好了。
t_en: It would be wonderful if you learned of Hanunue's life from it.

t_ch: 我这边还有关于那个游戏的「纪念品」，如果你感兴趣，我们之后还可以聊聊。
t_en: I still have a souvenir for that game here. I can talk about it if you'd like.

t_ch: 朋友，好好逛逛流梦礁吧，这里藏着家族不愿展露给世人的东西…和我希望你能看到的东西。
t_en: My friend, be sure to make the most of your stroll around the Dreamflux Reef. There are hidden things that The Family does not wish for the world to see... as well as things I hope you will see.

t_ch: 我想跟你聊聊…
t_en: I'd like to chat with you...

t_ch: 嗯，想聊点什么？
t_en: Mm, what do you wish to talk about?

t_ch: 关于你的过去…
t_en: About your past...

t_ch: 我？我只是一个理应无名的陌生人，有着一段无聊的过去。
t_en: Me? I'm just a stranger who deserves no name, someone with a meaningless past.

t_ch: 我曾是梦主的鹰犬，为家族撕咬着为害梦境的蠹虫，以猎犬的身份为荣…直到擢升音级的「升音礼」过后，我从家主口中听到了哈努努这个名字。
t_en: I was once a hunting Hound of the Dreammaster, biting down on the enemies of The Family who would harm the Dreamscape. I reveled in the glory of the Hounds... until after the Tuneriser ceremony in which I was to receive a promotion, when I heard Hanunue's name from The Family Head.

t_ch: 我当时十分好奇，这个猎犬家系与匹诺康尼的成立者究竟是什么人？我问了我那狼影中的家主，但他却以沉默为应。
t_en: I was restlessly curious: What kind of a person was he? This founder of both the Bloodhound Family and Penacony? I asked The Family Head draped in wolven shadows, but my request was met with silence.

t_ch: 我问了很多人，有人说他是滥杀无辜的狂徒，有人说他是残暴不仁的昏君，有人说他是功过参半的庸人。
t_en: I asked many people, with some saying that he was a madman who massacred innocents and others who said he was a vehemently tyrannical ruler. Some even said he was a hopelessly average man who did as much harm as he did good.

t_ch: 我的家族世代都是猎犬，我以猎犬为荣，绝不能接受猎犬的创立者是一个罪人……
t_en: My family has been Hounds for generations. I take pride in being a Hound, and I would never accept that the Hounds' founder was a sinner...

t_ch: ——于是我一路探寻真相，来到了流梦礁，见到了与哈努努为伴的那位「钟表匠」。听他讲述了过去的事情，我才找到答案…至少在我这里，他是个英雄。
t_en: ...And so, I relentlessly sought the truth, arriving at the Dreamflux Reef, and met with that friend of Hanunue's — the Watchmaker. Upon hearing him narrate the past stories, I finally found the answer... he was a hero, at least in my heart.

t_ch: 关于哈努努…
t_en: About Hanunue...

t_ch: 哈努努…匹诺康尼的缔造者，猎犬家系的第一任家主，这片梦境成为美梦之前的主人。
t_en: Hanunue... Penacony's founder, the Bloodhound Family's first head, and the master of this Dreamscape before it became a beautiful dream.

t_ch: 他已经逝去了数百年，曾为我带来过困惑，也曾令我陷入迷茫。但现在，他是我的精神榜样。
t_en: He has been dead for centuries. He once brought me vexation and caused me confusion, but now he is a role model that I emulate in spirit.

t_ch: 食鳄的狂狼、噩梦的暴君、阿斯德纳的尖牙…他的称号有很多，但我觉得最合适的只有一个——「反抗者」。
t_en: The Croc-eating Mad Wolf, Tyrant of Nightmares, Asdana's Fang... He has many names, but I think there is only one title that befits him — Rebel.

t_ch: 他反抗侵占自己家园的寡头，反抗困住自己脚步的公司，反抗一切可以反抗的东西。他是一种精神，只可惜…这种精神正在如今的梦中逐渐消散。
t_en: He rebelled against the oligarchs who invaded his home, resisted the IPC that restricted his progress, and fought against all that he could fight against. He is an ideal materialized. Alas... this ideal is fading in the current dream.

t_ch: 关于哈努兄弟…
t_en: About Brother Hanu...

t_ch: 呵，他大概是《钟表小子》中最没有悬念的卡通人物，因为「钟表匠」把哈努努的名字直接写在了上面。
t_en: Heh, he's probably the character with the least suspense in the Clockie cartoon, because the Watchmaker wrote Hanunue's name on it.

t_ch: 有人觉得这是在娱乐这位英雄和过去的苦难…我起初也很反感，但后来才发现，要想向别人诉说那些故事，最适合匹诺康尼的就是这种做法。
t_en: Some think this is deriving entertainment from this hero and the suffering of the past... Initially, I was very averse to this as well, but I later realized that if we were to narrate such stories to others, this would be the most Penaconian way to do so.

t_ch: 宾客朝欢暮乐，逐梦客只知名利，我们家徒也都只是沉浸于狂信和愚忠…没人会在意那些已经逝去的人与事，所以不如就配合这场狂欢，讲荒诞的故事。
t_en: The guests revel in joys from dusk till dawn and dreamchasers only chase after fame. Even Family members like us only have ears for the fanatical faith, pledging ourselves with foolish loyalty... No one cares for the people and the matters that are buried with the dead. So, why not join the crowd in this carnival and narrate the stories of absurdity?

t_ch: 咳…说实话，写《小小哈努兄弟》的时候，我还挺开心的。
t_en: Ahem... Truth be told, I was quite happy when I wrote Lil' Brother Hanu.

t_ch: 如果我不回到猎犬家系，而是在这里创作哈努兄弟故事，似乎也是一种不错的生活……
t_en: It doesn't sound like a bad way of life if I don't return to the Bloodhound Family and instead stay here to create Brother Hanu's story...

t_ch: 没什么想问的了。
t_en: That's all the questions I have.

t_ch: 我想要哈努兄弟的纪念品。
t_en: I want a Brother Hanu souvenir.

t_ch: 呵呵，那东西还没做好呢，以后再来吧。
t_en: Hehe, it's not made yet. Come back next time.

t_ch: 不过…你如果想买下它的话，要准备好「代价」哦。
t_en: But... if you wish to buy it, you must be prepared to pay the "price."

t_ch: 嗯，你想要吗？
t_en: Oh, you want it?

t_ch: 也不是不行，不过它的价格可不便宜……
t_en: I mean, you can have it, but it's not exactly cheap...

t_ch: 我的信用点多的是！
t_en: I have more than enough credits!

t_ch: 呵呵，我不要苜蓿币和信用点。
t_en: Hehe, I don't want Alfalfa credits or IPC credits.

t_ch: 不会要用星琼吧…
t_en: You can't possibly want Stellar Jades, do you...?

t_ch: 嗯？那是什么东西？
t_en: Eh? What are those?

t_ch: 要想带走这份纪念品，就请给我你的「黄金限定•艾迪恩代币」吧，那东西在某个「时刻」能派上想不到的用处。
t_en: If you want to take this souvenir with you, you'll have to give me your Aideen Token (Golden Limited Edition). That item will become useful in a way beyond your imagination in a certain "Moment."

t_ch: 好，那这份纪念品就给你了——
t_en: All right, I'll give you this souvenir...

t_ch: 把它摆在你的据点吧，你可以随时用它变成哈努兄弟，重温你曾经的冒险…也是那位英雄的故事。
t_en: Show it off in your stronghold. You can use it to turn into Brother Hanu whenever you like and relive your past adventures... In other words, the story of that hero.

t_ch: 呵，没关系，我会等有缘人把它带走的。
t_en: Heh, no matter. I will wait for a destined individual to take it away for me.

t_ch: 祝你玩得开心，也愿你不要忘记哈努努的事迹。
t_en: Happy times to you, and may you never forget Hanunue's deeds.

t_ch: 再见。
t_en: Goodbye.

t_ch: 再见，如果想聊哈努努，随时可以来找我。
t_en: Farewell. If you wish to talk about Hanunue, I'm more than welcome to entertain you anytime.

t_ch: 嗯…你在改变我的情绪，对吗？
t_en: Hmm... You're changing my emotions, aren't you?

t_ch: 「钟表匠」的小把戏对我这个级别的家徒并不好用。如果你想了解我，可以不必采取这种方式。
t_en: The Watchmaker's paltry tricks are no match for a Family member of my level. You don't have to resort to such antics if you want to learn about me.

t_ch: 那就来聊点让我感觉平静的话题吧——关于我的身世。
t_en: Let's talk about topics that calm me then — like where I come from.

t_ch: 我出自世代侍奉家族的血脉，因为我的先祖是一位「第Ⅱ音级」的大家长，亲迎过{RUBY_B#「同谐」的化身}「无限夫长」{RUBY_E#}的意志。
t_en: I was born into a bloodline that has served The Family for generations, because my ancestor was a scale degree II grand guardian, and had personally ushered in the will of the {RUBY_B#Embodiment of Harmony} Eternal Centurion{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 我从出生以来就在听从「同谐」的教诲，与兄弟姐妹们服务于各个分家，从未有过二心……
t_en: I obeyed Harmony's teachings since I came to this world and served the various clans with my siblings, never once betraying them...

t_ch: 所以当我来到流梦礁，看到自己信仰被人贬低、侮辱、憎恶时，我十分愤怒，但当了解了这里发生的一切，我居然瞬间释怀了！
t_en: So, when I came to the Dreamflux Reef and saw that my own faith was derided, insulted, and hated, I was enraged. But when I understood all that had happened here, I was suddenly enlightened!

t_ch: 我觉得我并没有背叛同谐，反倒是同谐让我接纳了这一切…我认为无论是「钟表匠」还是哈努努，他们渴望的都是一个安宁无忧的乐园，这也是家族所期望的。
t_en: I don't think I betrayed Harmony. In fact, it is Harmony that made me to accept all this... Whether it be the Watchmaker or Hanunue, they all simply desired a peaceful haven free of troubles, which is also what The Family hopes for.

t_ch: 大家的梦想一样，却因立场兵戎相见，真是可惜呀……
t_en: Everyone's dreams are the same, yet we became foes due to our differing allegiances. How unfortunate...

t_ch: 嗯…你在改变我的情绪，对吗？
t_en: Hmm... You're changing my emotions, aren't you?

t_ch: 「钟表匠」的小把戏对我这个级别的家徒并不好用。如果你想了解我，可以不必采取这种方式。
t_en: The Watchmaker's paltry tricks are no match for a Family member of my level. You don't have to resort to such antics if you want to learn about me.

t_ch: 那就来聊点让我感觉开心的话题吧——现在有越来越多的人想了解匹诺康尼的过去了。
t_en: Let's talk about something that makes me happy, then — there are more people nowadays who wish to learn about Penacony's past.

t_ch: 或许是「钟表匠」的动画起了作用，或许是如今宇宙中的有识之士越来越多…总而言之，这是个好兆头。
t_en: Perhaps the Watchmaker's cartoons are working, or that there are more knowledgeable individuals in the cosmos nowadays... All in all, this is a good start.

t_ch: 对于哈努努，我也有很多不了解的地方，他的故事实在太长，「钟表匠」生前并没有跟我讲完…所以，我会和那些好奇的人一起去探寻。
t_en: There are many things I don't understand about Hanunue, either. His story is too long, and the Watchmaker did not finish telling me about them when he was alive... Which is why I will find it with those who are curious.

t_ch: 朋友，你应该也是我们的一员吧——那就去看看，去「开拓」那些不为人知的故事吧。
t_en: Friend, you should be one of us as well, right? Then let's go check it out together. Let's "trailblaze" upon those unknown stories.

t_ch: 嗯…你在改变我的情绪，对吗？
t_en: Hmm... You're changing my emotions, aren't you?

t_ch: 「钟表匠」的小把戏对我这个级别的家徒并不好用。如果你想了解我，可以不必采取这种方式。
t_en: The Watchmaker's paltry tricks are no match for a Family member of my level. You don't have to resort to such antics if you want to learn about me.

t_ch: 那就来聊点让我感觉愤怒的话题吧——现在关于哈努努的谣言越来越多了。
t_en: Let's talk about something that makes me angry, then — such as how there are more and more rumors about Hanunue these days.

t_ch: 哈努努确实不是一位完美的领导者。他在统治匹诺康尼期间犯下了许多错误，也让一些无辜的生命消逝……
t_en: Honestly, Hanunue was not a perfect leader. He made many mistakes while governing Penacony, resulting in the loss of many innocent lives...

t_ch: 但他的勇敢与反抗拯救了更多的人，不该被简单地判定为罪人。
t_en: But his courage and resistance saved even more people. He should not have been simply branded as a criminal.

t_ch: 在我们现在能接触到的史料里，哈努努的过错被无限放大，仿佛是他让匹诺康尼陷入绝境，才有了家族救世的传奇，这太荒谬了！
t_en: Hanunue's mistakes were amplified exponentially in the history books that we have access to these days, as if he was the one who created Penacony's crisis, paving the way for The Family's legendary rescue of this world. Preposterous!

t_ch: 所以，我要把哈努努的英雄事迹传遍梦境，当大家知道了这些故事，才能给他一个公正的判词！
t_en: This is why I wish to spread Hanunue's heroic deeds across the Dreamscape. The people will only give him a fair judgment when everyone becomes aware of these stories!

t_ch: 嗯…你在改变我的情绪，对吗？
t_en: Hmm... You're changing my emotions, aren't you?

t_ch: 「钟表匠」的小把戏对我这个级别的家徒并不好用。如果你想了解我，可以不必采取这种方式。
t_en: The Watchmaker's paltry tricks are no match for a Family member of my level. You don't have to resort to such antics if you want to learn about me.

t_ch: 那就来聊点让我感觉悲伤的话题吧——曾经和哈努努并肩作战的人，已经所剩无几了，那千年前的故事还有几位亲历者呢？
t_en: Let's talk about something that makes me sad, then — there aren't many left out of those who once fought beside Hanunue. How many eyewitnesses do we have left for even older stories, then? For stories that happened thousands of years ago?

t_ch: 即便动用梦境的力量将自己转化为迷因，并非忆者的凡人也终究会在「记忆」的遗忘中衰败，在「神秘」的磨损中逐渐消散，就像「钟表匠」的一样悄然离去。
t_en: Even if one turns into a memetic entity with the power of the Dreamscape, those who aren't Memokeepers will inevitably wane in how the Remembrance forgets, gradually dissolve in the corrosion of the Enigmata, and quietly depart from existence just like the Watchmaker did.

t_ch: 生命真是太短暂了，与生命相伴的过去真是太脆弱了。丰饶的追随者们用不朽的身体抵御岁月，记忆的追随者们将岁月封录成册，学会的烛墨学者四处求索记录……
t_en: Life is far too short, and the past that walks shoulder to shoulder with such lives is far too fragile. The followers of Abundance use their timeless corpus to resist time, the followers of Remembrance seal the past into volumes, and the Candelagraphos scholars of the Guild hurry everywhere to seek records...

t_ch: 但即便如此，他们也没法拯救所有的过去，与跟随过去一同消损的英雄。
t_en: But even so, they cannot salvage all of the past, nor save the heroes who perished together with bygone times.

t_ch: 朋友，这也是为什么我要跟你讲哈努努的故事，因为只有「传承」，才能抵御岁月的流逝……
t_en: Friend, this is why I need to tell you Hanunue's story. Only by telling the past to the next generation can we resist the flow of time...

t_ch: 有人注视着你——
t_en: Someone's watching you...

t_ch: 那目光平静、专注、不含恶意，却仍令你芒刺在背。
t_en: Their gaze is calm, focused, and devoid of ill intentions. Nevertheless, it makes you feel nervous and uneasy.

t_ch: 你转过头，正对上金属面庞上一双难以分辨的眼睛。
t_en: You look back and stare straight into a pair of inscrutable eyes sitting upon a metallic face.

t_ch: …您可真美。
t_en: You're so... gorgeous.

t_ch: 呃…谢谢夸奖？
t_en: Ah... thank you?

t_ch: 这算被骚扰了吗？
t_en: Is this considered harassment?

t_ch: 我也觉得我很美！
t_en: I find myself gorgeous, too!

t_ch: 古怪的智械似乎并不在乎你作何反应。他的目光似乎落在你身上，又似乎不在。
t_en: The strange Intellitron does not seem to care about your response. You cannot tell if he is actually looking at you.

t_ch: 那是种近乎玄妙的感觉。他的目光仿佛正缓缓渗透你的肌肤、血肉、骨骼乃至灵魂，悄无声息地探寻着某种更加抽象、更加纯粹的「本质」。
t_en: What a strange feeling. His gaze is so deep that you feel it seep into your skin, past your flesh, your bones, and into your soul. Silently, it searches for your inherent "nature" in its most abstract and purest form.

t_ch: …您身上有「毁灭」的气息，却又兼具「存护」的意志。二者交汇相融，保持着某种微妙的平衡…我曾在一位虔诚的{RUBY_B#「均衡信徒」}仲裁官{RUBY_E#}身上窥见过相似的美感。
t_en: There is an air of Destruction about you, yet you possess the will of the Preservation. Both Paths are able to maintain a delicate balance despite their convergence... I have seen a similar kind of beauty before, when I gazed upon the body of a pious {RUBY_B# Follower of the Equilibrium}Arbitrator{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 除此之外…除此之外还有同谐的韵律，就像黑白之外又沾染、晕开暧昧的色彩…这非常美。
t_en: In addition... in addition to that, you possess the rhythm of the Harmony, like a monochrome shade that has been stained with a dizzying array of enigmatic hues. Such immense beauty, indeed.

t_ch: 真想让您成为我的模特……
t_en: I really want you to be my model...

t_ch: 你怎么知道…
t_en: How did you know...

t_ch: 我是名摄影师，眼睛就是我的镜头——无论字面意义或是比喻意义都如此。
t_en: As a photographer, my eyes serve as the lens of my camera, both literally and metaphorically.

t_ch: 也不是不行。
t_en: I'm fine with that.

t_ch: 感谢您的慷慨，我会铭记您的承诺。
t_en: Thank you for your generosity. I will always remember your promise to me.

t_ch: 我拒绝。
t_en: I refuse.

t_ch: 请别急着拒绝，现在尚且为时过早……
t_en: It's too early for you to reject me just yet...

t_ch: 我有种预感——您将来还会染上更多色彩，它们或许会令您无比强大、又或者走向毁灭。
t_en: I have a premonition that you will be stained with even more shades of color in the future. These hues will either grant you strength beyond compare or steer you toward destruction.

t_ch: 如果真有那么一天，希望我能赶到您的身边，记录下您那一刻无与伦比的「美丽」。
t_en: If such a day ultimately occurs, I hope that you will hurry back to me, so that I can record your incomparable "beauty" in that moment.

t_ch: …您又来了。
t_en: You're back.

t_ch: 您对我的作品感兴趣吗？
t_en: Are you interested in my works?

t_ch: 不日我即将在「蓝调的时刻」举办个人摄影展览，如果您有时间，可以来看看。
t_en: Soon, I shall hold a personal photo exhibition in the Blue Hour. Do visit it if you have the time.

t_ch: 「如果面前的智械有双碳基生物的眼睛……」
t_en: "If the Intellitron in front of me possessed a pair of living eyes..."

t_ch: 你不知为何，开始忍不住这般想象起来——
t_en: For some unknown reason, you can't help but start imagining.

t_ch: 那或许会是双漂亮的蓝眼睛，就像黎明来临前的夜空。
t_en: Perhaps his eyes would be brilliantly blue, like the shade of a night sky right before dawn.

t_ch: 或许清澈的虹膜流光溢彩，倒映出色彩缤纷的梦境。
t_en: Or perhaps his clear irises would be full of radiance that reflect the vibrant colors of the Dreamscape.

t_ch: 而那层泡沫般的光影之下，更深处根本空无一物……
t_en: However, beneath that bubbly shadow, there is only emptiness...

t_ch: 智械的目光似乎落在你身上，又似乎不在。
t_en: You cannot tell if the Intellitron is actually looking at you.

t_ch: 你分辨不清他的表情，却直觉般感受到那平静之下的冷淡与挑剔。
t_en: Even though you can't place his expression, you instinctively feel a sense of apathy and criticism beneath his calm demeanor.

t_ch: 就仿佛…在对方眼中，你只是个尚不完美的半成品。
t_en: As though... you are but an imperfect work in his eyes.

t_ch: …您想与我聊聊吗？
t_en: Care to chat with me?

t_ch: 我曾遇见过一位「美丽」的作品。
t_en: I once encountered someone who was a work of absolute "beauty."

t_ch: 是的，一位，我所使用的量词准确无误。
t_en: You heard me right. Precisely one person, who was the epitome of beauty.

t_ch: 她曾是名混沌医师，却最终因为「虚无」的侵蚀成为了自灭者。
t_en: Even though she used to be a member of the Doctors of Chaos, she ultimately became a Self-Annihilator due to the corrosion of the Nihility.

t_ch: 我们相遇于她人生的末尾，那时她早已因丧失理智而深陷谵妄与幻觉。
t_en: I met her near the end of her life. By then, she had long since lost her sanity and sank into a mix of delirium and hallucinations.

t_ch: 我目睹了她如何将自己献祭给虚无——
t_en: I witnessed how she offered herself to the Nihility...

t_ch: 她将自己制作成精美的人偶，与一具具同样工艺制成的人偶共同悬吊在半空。
t_en: She crafted herself into an exquisite puppet and hung in midair, alongside other puppets that had been crafted the exact same way.

t_ch: 他们曾是她的伙伴、她的病人，而那时他们依旧如生前般簇拥着她。
t_en: These puppets were her erstwhile companions and patients once. They all surrounded her, just as they did while she was still alive.

t_ch: 我看见他们的头颅绽放，猩红玫瑰花瓣在星光与灯光间如此娇艳欲滴……
t_en: I saw roses bloom from their heads, the petals vivid shades of crimson amid the glow of starlight...

t_ch: 我的镜头——我的眼睛——将那一刻记录了下来。从那时起，我的逻辑模块出现了无法修复的错误。
t_en: My lens — my eyes — recorded that moment. From then on, my logic modules have been beset with irreparable errors.

t_ch: 他们都说我被污染了…或许吧，但这于我而言大概并不算一件坏事。
t_en: They all said that I had been corrupted... Perhaps, but this wasn't a bad thing to happen in my eyes.

t_ch: 我因此获得了「自由」，开始寻求某种自己发自内心渴望着的「美」。
t_en: After all, that incident had granted me "freedom." Since then, I have been searching for the "beauty" that I crave from the bottom of my heart.

t_ch: 我曾拜访过某个阴郁的墓场，那里每座墓碑旁都有连接着铰链的钟塔，我逐个鸣钟，想知道究竟是否有人被过早埋葬；
t_en: I once visited a gloomy graveyard. Every tombstone in that graveyard was linked to a hinged bell. I rang each bell one by one, wanting to know whether anyone had been prematurely buried.

t_ch: 我曾探索过一片纯白的沼泽，那里的生物只要沾染过沼泽中的白泥浆，就会因为污染而渐渐化作枯败僵硬的灰白泥塑；
t_en: Another time, I explored a completely white swamp. There, any organism that came into contact with the swamp's white mud would gradually turn into a withered and stiff ashen clay sculpture due to the contamination.

t_ch: 我曾去过太多地方…在死与恶中，嗅到无限生机、窥见永恒的美。于是我将它们记录下来。
t_en: I've been to too many places... Amidst death and evil, I have discovered limitless opportunity and observed eternal beauty. This is why I record such moments down.

t_ch: 用我的眼睛…用我的镜头……
t_en: With my eyes... With my lens...

t_ch: 我曾去过太多地方…在死与恶中，嗅到无限生机、窥见永恒的美。于是我将它们记录下来。
t_en: I've been to too many places... Amidst death and evil, I have discovered limitless opportunity and observed eternal beauty. This is why I record such moments down.

t_ch: 用我的眼睛…用我的镜头……
t_en: With my eyes... With my lens...

t_ch: 可以聊聊吗？
t_en: Are you up for a chat?

t_ch: 男人没有回答，只是平静地注视着你。但你明白这并非拒绝。
t_en: The man does not reply and continues to observe you calmly. However, you know that he's not rejecting your request.

t_ch: 你是什么人？
t_en: Who are you?

t_ch: 一个摄影师。
t_en: A photographer.

t_ch: 有些人称呼我为「虚无的摄影师」，但我自认并未走上「虚无」的命途——至少并非在通常意义上。
t_en: Some call me the Nihility's Photographer, but I do not see myself as having walked down the Path of Nihility... At least, not in the usual sense.

t_ch: 我只是个忠实的记录者，记录万物毁坏湮灭那一刻所绽放出的「美」。
t_en: I am but a faithful record keeper who captures the moments of "beauty" that are born from the destruction and obliteration of all things.

t_ch: 你很出名吗？
t_en: Are you famous?

t_ch: 也许吧。真正的「美」并不面向大众，但总归有人会理解。
t_en: Perhaps. Although true beauty does not conform to the masses, there will always be people who are able to understand its meaning.

t_ch: 当然，名声对我而言并无意义——除非它能为我提供拍摄素材。
t_en: Naturally, fame is meaningless to me, unless it can aid me in acquiring more source material to capture.

t_ch: 你忙吧，不打扰了。
t_en: I'll let you get back to your work.

t_ch: 智械沉默着点头，权当作别。
t_en: The Intellitron nods silently in response. Perhaps that was his way of bidding farewell.

t_ch: 就这样吧，拜拜。
t_en: That's all. Bye-bye.

t_ch: 古怪的智械并未回应你的道别，仅以目光送别你远去的背影。
t_en: The strange Intellitron does not respond to your farewell, and merely gazes at you as you walk away.

t_ch: 莱斯特先生，今晚您有空吗，我想请您……
t_en: Mr. Lester, are you free tonight? I wish to invite you to...

t_ch: 抱歉，美丽的女士，我已经有安排了。
t_en: Apologies, beautiful lady. I've already made plans for tonight.

t_ch: 那明晚……
t_en: Well then, how about tomorrow night...

t_ch: 他明晚也没空，阿姨你就不要再缠着人家了。莱斯特先生不喜欢你这个类型，要是实在不甘寂寞，你不如去问问旁边那位先生吧，你们瞧着倒是般配。
t_en: He's busy tomorrow night too. Old lady, why don't you give it a rest? Mr. Lester doesn't like women like you. If you're lonely, why don't you try that mister? You two look like a good match.

t_ch: 这种私人恩怨劳驾不要伤及无辜。这位小姐的失礼可真让人困扰…不如这样，莱斯特先生，我请您去安静的地方喝一杯？
t_en: Let's not involve others in our personal squabbles, shall we? This lady's impropriety is troubling indeed... How about a drink somewhere quieter, Mr. Lester?

t_ch: 抱歉，我酒精过敏……
t_en: Apologies, I'm allergic to alcohol.

t_ch: 说什么呢大叔，到底是谁没礼貌……
t_en: Hey, what did you say, mister? Who's the impolite one around here, I wonder?

t_ch: 诸位，请先冷静一些……
t_en: Everybody, please calm down...

t_ch: 就在这位名为莱斯特的男子一筹莫展之际，他的通讯器突然响起。
t_en: Just as this man named Lester is about to reach his wit's end, his communicator suddenly begins to ring.

t_ch: …这样啊…好的，我知道了，我会尽早处理的……
t_en: Hm... I see... Alright, I'll deal with it as soon as possible...

t_ch: 抱歉，诸位，我还有些工作要处理。
t_en: Apologies, there are some things that I need to take care of at present.

t_ch: 可是……
t_en: But...

t_ch: 与诸位聊天是件非常愉快的事，我们有缘再见。
t_en: I've had a great time talking to all of you. We'll meet again if destiny permits.

t_ch: 众人表情各异，一番踌躇后各自散去。莱斯特望着他们远去的背影，长舒口气。
t_en: After some hesitation, the rest of the assembled crowd take their leave, a motley of expressions on their faces. Lester sighs deeply as he gazes at their receding figures.

t_ch: 呼…人都走了，这次也多谢你帮我解围，克劳迪娅……
t_en: Whew... They've all left. I must thank you for helping me out again, Claudia.

t_ch: 你们也上船了？什么时候…好吧，那看好你二哥，顺便帮我探探他的口风……
t_en: You've boarded the ship as well? When did you... Alright. In that case, look after your second-eldest brother and try to find out more information from him.

t_ch: …没问题，你要的最新典藏版已经买好了…嗯，辛苦你了……
t_en: Not a problem. I've also bought the new Collector's Edition that you wanted. Right, thank you for your help...

t_ch: …嗯？您好，{F#美丽的小姐}{M#英俊的先生}，很高兴见到您。
t_en: ...Oh? Greetings, {F#beautiful lady}{M#handsome sir}. It's a pleasure to meet you.

t_ch: 抱歉我方才稍有些出神，但您瞧，这里的风景着实美丽宜人——
t_en: Sorry, I was lost in thought just now... but look! The view here is simply stunning.

t_ch: 即便是在梦中，高空的景致也总因未被人烟熏染，而显得格外清新宁静，让人不由想要自此沉睡下去……
t_en: Even in dreams, the scenery from above is especially serene because it has not been tainted by humans yet. Such landscapes can really make me quite sleepy...

t_ch: 倘若您此刻不忙，不妨一起来放松片刻。
t_en: If you're not busy, perhaps you'd like to relax with me for a brief moment?

t_ch: 男人半垂眼睑，苍白面庞在灯光与天光的辉映中显得格外落寞。
t_en: Basked in both natural and man-made light, the man's drooping eyelids and pale complexion make him seem especially lonely.

t_ch: …我偶尔会做些奇怪的梦——有时我缓缓沉入泥淖，喉头舌尖尽是铁锈味；有时我躺在狭窄逼仄的棺材里，被深埋泥土之下，而手边并没有连接钟塔的铰链……
t_en: Occasionally, I'll have some strange dreams... Once, I dreamed that I was slowly sinking into a bog, my whole mouth filled with the taste of rust. Another time, I dreamed that I was lying in a narrow coffin that had been buried deep underground, without any bell to ring...

t_ch: …我的母亲是位美丽的女士，她知性又优雅、独立而自由…她将我培养成为一位「绅士」，然后为了追求自由弃我而去……
t_en: My mother is a gorgeous lady. She's intelligent, elegant, independent, and uninhibited... After raising me as a gentleman, she abandoned me in pursuit of her own freedom.

t_ch: 我爱我的弟弟和妹妹——虽然我们的血脉不尽相同——他们也同样爱我…我们彼此信赖、相互帮扶，但我们之间永远有所隔阂……
t_en: Even though we are not related by blood, I love my siblings, and they love me too. We trust each other and help one another out wherever we can, but there will always be a gap between us...

t_ch: 「人与人永远无法相互理解」——我的母亲曾如此教导我…而时至今日我愈发深以为然。
t_en: My mother once taught me that there can be no mutual understanding between people. Even to this day, I am deeply convinced by her words.

t_ch: 或许这也是我之所以不喜欢匹诺康尼的原因。我的梦总是满载混乱与孤独，就像我迄今为止的人生一样。而这个人工构建的「美梦」……
t_en: Perhaps this is the reason why I dislike Penacony. My dreams are always full of chaos and loneliness, not unlike the life that I've lived thus far. As for this artificially designed "sweet dream"...

t_ch: 它不过是强行将人们连接在一起，勉强拼凑出欢乐美好的假象…我深谙它何其虚假，就如同我了解自己是多么的虚伪不堪。
t_en: It merely forces people together in a feeble attempt to create the illusion of bliss and happiness. I understand this is a fantasy, just as I am inherently aware of my own hypocrisy.

t_ch: 男人沉默地望向窗外，冷硬的玻璃平面倒映出他嘴角冷硬的弧度。
t_en: The man silently gazes outside, the chilly surface of the window's glass reflecting his stiff smile.

t_ch: …有时我不禁思考，人与人之间的社交，当真是必要的吗？
t_en: Sometimes, I can't help but wonder... are social interactions truly necessary?

t_ch: 在表达基础需求之外，又额外演化出一套如此复杂繁琐的交流体系…或许人们愿称之为文明的进步、文化的丰饶，但我只觉得聒噪。
t_en: The evolution of such a complicated and tedious communication system on top of merely expressing one's basic demands... Perhaps humans view this as the progress of civilization and the abundance of culture. Yet, I find such a system truly grating.

t_ch: 人们精心铺垫、委婉试探，乔装目的、巧饰修辞…可最终又收获了什么呢？
t_en: People carefully pave the way for tactful probing, concealing their goals and disguising their rhetoric... but to what end?

t_ch: 或许很多时候他们确实得到了自己想要的…但说实话，比起在达成目的的过程中那些徒劳无益的消耗，这又何尝不是种得不偿失……
t_en: While they may get what they desire most of the time, truth be told... when compared to the futile effort spent on achieving their objectives, such losses do outweigh the gains, am I right?

t_ch: 请原谅我如此失礼，但可否让我一个人安静会儿？
t_en: Please excuse my lack of manners, but could you leave me be for a while?

t_ch: 机会难得，得好好规划才行……
t_en: Since this is such a rare opportunity, I must come up with a detailed plan...

t_ch: …嗯，我记得那场拍卖有克劳迪娅想要的手稿…咦，这个雕塑…路易斯是不是最近才给它的创作者写过评论……
t_en: Mmm, I remember that the manuscript which Claudia wants is at that auction... Huh? This statue... Didn't Louis just recently critique its sculptor?

t_ch: 那就把「薄暮的时刻」列入行程…咦，「太阳的时刻」好像也不错——博物馆和图书馆，两个小家伙应该很乐意在那里打发时间……
t_en: In that case, I'll add the Moment of Dusk to the itinerary. Huh, seems like the Moment of Sol is not a bad option as well. A museum and a library... The two little ones should be quite willing to pass the time there.

t_ch: 或许「绿洲的时刻」也是个好的选择？我们已经很久没有一起野餐了，偶尔晒晒太阳、放空一下也不错。嗯，到时候干脆雇佣一只气泡锂犬吧，我记得路易斯小时候最喜欢玩飞盘游戏了……
t_en: Or perhaps the Moment of Oasis would be a good option as well? Since we haven't picnicked in so long, basking in the sun and relaxing once in a while sounds like a good idea. Ahh, on that day, I'll hire a Bubble Hound. I recall that Louis loved playing holodisc when he was young...

t_ch: 至于其它的…餐厅？大剧院？影视乐园或者…算了，这些都过于普通，他们一定不会喜欢。
t_en: Apart from that... How about a restaurant? Or the Grand Theater? Or Clock Studios Theme Park? Or... forget it. They wouldn't like these places since they're so ordinary.
